title: lupinus vallicola var. apricus
intro: Lupinus vallicola var. apricus, also known as the apricot lupine, is a variety of the lupine plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial legume that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, due to its high content of alkaloids and saponins. Lupinus vallicola var. apricus can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and has been traditionally used by indigenous communities to treat various ailments.
